article_meta.title: Sign up for Executive Dysfunction, a weekly newsletter that surfaces under-the-radar stories about what Trump is doing t

reasoning: Mixed. The model correctly flagged several biased phrases in the author’s opening prose, but it also flagged multiple statements that belong to the Q&A (attributed to the interviewer/interviewee) as author bias. It also missed a few clear biased phrasings in the author’s own narration.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Three of six flagged items come from the interview/Q&A section (attributed speakers), violating the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: About half of the flagged items are not the author’s own prose, making them false positives for author-level bias.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed defensible author-prose bias such as 'of course,' 'freewheeling authority,' and 'startling claim of unilateral war powers.'
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: For the correctly attributed author-prose items, labels were reasonable (sensationalism, opinion-as-fact, slant).
